question: Based on the chart, did total sales in the first half of the year exceed those in the second half?

options.A: No, the second half of the year had higher total sales.
options.B: Yes, the first half of the year had higher total sales.
options.C: Both halves of the year had equal total sales.
options.D: The chart does not provide enough information to compare the two halves.

explanation: The months on the x‑axis are arranged in reverse (Dec back to Jan), so the filled area shape is misleading if read left‑to‑right. When you re‑order the months to the natural calendar sequence and sum Jan–Jun versus Jul–Dec, you find that the first half actually has higher total sales. The inappropriate month order can easily invert this interpretation.
